original_text: R&D investment intensity in Q4 2025 was 17.73% of revenue (K007), with R&D expenses of $1,098,000,000 (C016) on revenue of $6,194,000,000 (C010). This compares to $1,088,000,000 in Q3 2025 (C024), $1,082,000,000 in Q2 2025 (C032), and $1,110,000,000 in Q1 2026 (C008), suggesting sustained commitment to product development despite revenue fluctuations. The consistent R&D spending level, representing approximately one-sixth of revenue, indicates Adobe prioritizes innovation and product enhancement, which is critical for maintaining competitive positioning in the creative software and digital experience markets.
revised_text: R&D investment intensity in Q4 2025 was 17.73% of revenue (K007), with R&D expenses of $1,098,000,000 (C018) on revenue of $6,194,000,000 (C012). This compares to $1,088,000,000 in Q3 2025 (C026) and $1,082,000,000 in Q2 2025 (C034), suggesting sustained commitment to product development despite revenue fluctuations. The consistent R&D spending level indicates Adobe prioritizes innovation and product enhancement, which is critical for maintaining competitive positioning in the creative software and digital experience markets.
changes_summary: Fixed five citation mismatches: C016→C018 for Q4 R&D, C010→C012 for Q4 revenue, C024→C026 for Q3 R&D, C032→C034 for Q2 R&D, and removed Q1 2026 reference (C008→C010) due to critical scope violation (future period beyond 2025-08 as-of date). Removed unsupported claim about R&D representing 'approximately one-sixth of revenue' as actual ratios range from 17.35%-18.42%, not 16.67%.
